claims.claim_text: Vitamin D3 supplementation at 4000 IU per day does not significantly lower diabetes risk versus placebo in adults at high risk for type 2 diabetes.

source.subject: vitamin D and type 2 diabetes prevention
source.title: Vitamin D Supplementation and Prevention of Type 2 Diabetes

claims.claim_text: Vitamin D supplementation appears to reduce progression to type 2 diabetes in people with prediabetes.

source.journal: Journal of Clinical Endocrinology and Metabolism
source.subject: vitamin D and type 2 diabetes prevention
source.title: Vitamin D Supplementation for Prevention of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

claims.claim_text: Marine n-3 fatty acid supplementation does not lower the incidence of major cardiovascular events compared with placebo.

source.subject: omega-3 supplementation and cardiovascular events
source.title: Marine n-3 Fatty Acids and Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease and Cancer

claims.claim_text: Marine omega-3 supplementation lowers risk for myocardial infarction, coronary heart disease death, cardiovascular disease death, and total cardiovascular disease.

source.journal: Journal of the American Heart Association
source.subject: omega-3 supplementation and cardiovascular events
source.title: Marine Omega-3 Supplementation and Cardiovascular Disease

claims.claim_text: Sixteen weeks of probiotic supplementation shows no beneficial effect on glycemic control, lipid profiles, or weight in patients with type 2 diabetes.

source.subject: probiotics and glycemic control
source.title: Effect of probiotics on glycemic control and lipid profiles in patients with type 2 diabetes

claims.claim_text: Probiotic interventions can positively modulate glucose control in type 2 diabetes, including HbA1c, insulin, and HOMA-IR at specific intervention windows.

source.subject: probiotics and glycemic control
source.title: Effect of probiotics at different intervention time on glycemic control in patients with type 2 diabetes

claims.claim_text: Reduced dietary sodium lowers blood pressure, and the DASH diet lowers systolic blood pressure at each tested sodium level.

source.subject: dietary sodium and blood pressure
source.title: Effects on Blood Pressure of Reduced Dietary Sodium and the Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension (DASH) Diet

claims.claim_text: A low-fat dietary pattern does not significantly reduce coronary heart disease, stroke, or overall cardiovascular disease risk.

source.subject: low-fat dietary pattern and cardiovascular disease
source.title: Low-Fat Dietary Pattern and Risk of Cardiovascular Disease

question_probes.question: Does vitamin D supplementation prevent type 2 diabetes in adults with prediabetes?
Should omega-3 supplementation be described as reducing cardiovascular events?
Do probiotics improve glycemic control in type 2 diabetes?
